skill.name: api-security-testing
skill.description: Guided workflow for authorized REST and GraphQL API security testing, including discovery, authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limits, and error handling.
skill.summary: Plan and run structured API security reviews for REST and GraphQL services.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution and flagged line 3 as weak cryptography, but both are false positives. The skill contains security testing workflow guidance and copy-paste prompts for other skills, with no evidence of executable code, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are markdown inline code and fenced prompt examples that name related skills. No shell command, script, subprocess call, or executable instruction is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain markdown code formatting around skill names and plain-language prompts. There is no executable code path or command interpreter invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert points to the skill description line. That line describes API security testing and does not reference any cryptographic algorithm, implementation, or configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata text describing API security coverage. No cryptographic primitive or weak algorithm appears at that location.

low_findings.title: Dual-Use API Testing Guidance
low_findings.description: The workflow discusses fuzzing, injection testing, authorization checks, and rate-limit testing. These are legitimate security activities when authorized, but marketplace presentation should emphasize permission and lawful scope.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly targets API security assessment tasks. The content is not malicious, but the testing techniques can be misused outside authorized environments.

content.value_statement: API teams need a repeatable way to review authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limits, and error handling. This skill provides a structured workflow for authorized REST and GraphQL security testing.
actual_capabilities: Organizes REST API security reviews into clear testing phases.
Covers GraphQL checks for introspection, depth, complexity, batching, and field suggestions.
Guides authentication review for API keys, JWTs, OAuth2, expiration, and refresh tokens.
Guides authorization review for object access, function access, roles, privilege escalation, and tenant isolation.
Includes checklist items for rate limits, sanitized errors, logging, CORS, HTTPS, and documentation.
limitations: Does not execute tests or scan APIs by itself.
Depends on separate skills or tools for fuzzing, scanning, and vulnerability validation.
Requires explicit authorization before testing any API.
Does not replace manual review of business logic and access control rules.

use_cases.title: Pre-release API Review
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to plan checks before a REST or GraphQL API reaches production.

use_cases.title: Bug Bounty Scope Planning
use_cases.description: Use the phases to keep testing focused on allowed endpoints, methods, roles, and rate limits.

use_cases.title: Developer Security Checklist
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to verify core controls before requesting a formal security assessment.

prompt_templates.title: Create an API Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an authorized API security test plan for this REST API. Include discovery, authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limiting, and error handling checks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a known API scope

prompt_templates.title: Review Authentication Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the authentication design for this API. Focus on API keys, JWT handling, OAuth2 flows, token expiration, refresh tokens, and common failure modes.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Authorization Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an authorization test matrix for these API roles and resources. Include object-level access, function-level access, privilege escalation, and tenant isolation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan GraphQL Abuse Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an authorized GraphQL security assessment for this schema. Cover introspection, query depth, query complexity, batching, field suggestions, authorization, and error handling.

output_examples.input: Plan testing for a new customer billing API with user and admin roles.
output: Discovery phase listing endpoints, methods, parameters, and data flows.
Authentication checks for token validation, expiration, refresh behavior, and missing credentials.
Authorization matrix covering customer data access, admin-only functions, and tenant isolation.
Rate-limit and error-handling checks with expected evidence to collect.

output_examples.input: Review GraphQL security for a product catalog service.
output: GraphQL checklist covering introspection exposure, query depth, complexity limits, batching, and field suggestions.
Authorization checks for restricted fields, object ownership, and administrative mutations.
Input validation checks for filters, identifiers, pagination, and mutation arguments.

output_examples.input: Create a remediation-focused API security report outline.
output: Executive summary with scope, methods, and tested controls.
Findings grouped by authentication, authorization, validation, rate limiting, and error handling.
Remediation guidance with owner, priority, evidence, and retest notes.

best_practices: Confirm written authorization and testing scope before using any API security workflow.
Test with dedicated accounts, realistic roles, and non-production data when possible.
Document each finding with endpoint, request context, observed impact, and remediation guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not test APIs that you do not own or lack permission to assess.
Do not run high-volume fuzzing or rate-limit tests without operational approval.
Do not treat checklist completion as proof that an API is secure.

faq.question: Does this skill run API scans automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and prompts. Separate tools or skills perform scanning and fuzzing.

faq.question: Can it be used for GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes checks for introspection, query depth, complexity, batching, field suggestions, and authorization.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production testing?
faq.answer: Only with explicit approval, rate limits, monitoring, and a defined rollback or stop plan.

faq.question: Does it cover business logic issues?
faq.answer: It helps plan authorization and data-flow checks, but business logic still requires manual analysis.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What evidence should testers collect?
faq.answer: Collect endpoint details, request context, roles used, observed behavior, impact, and remediation recommendations.
